content.problemStatement:
The XOR pair representation (XPR) of a positive integer NNN is defined as a pair of integers (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) such that:


1≤A≤B≤N1 \le A \le B \le N1≤A≤B≤N
A⊕B=NA \oplus B = NA⊕B=N
if there is no way to choose AAA and BBB satisfying the above conditions, A=B=−1A = B = -1A=B=−1
otherwise, the value of AAA should be the smallest possible
These conditions uniquely define the XPR. Next, we define a function F(N)F(N)F(N) = the value of BBB in XPR(N)XPR(N)XPR(N), and a function G(L,R)=∑i=LRF(i)G(L, R) = \sum\limits_{i=L}^R F(i)G(L,R)=i=L∑R​F(i).

You are given LLL and RRR. Compute G(L,R)G(L, R)G(L,R).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the value of the function G(L,R)G(L, R)G(L,R).
